title: oreosedum hirsutum
intro: Oreosedum hirsutum, also known as hairy rockrose or hairy sedum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Oreosedum hirsutum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Oreosedum hirsutum can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or topical applications.
